newsBriefing: The NBA news cycle tightened around health, history and deadline fallout: Ausar Thompson exited Detroit's loss in San Antonio with a right ankle sprain, Jayson Tatum is expected to make his season debut for Boston against Dallas after his Achilles recovery, and Memphis said Ja Morant will be out at least two more weeks with incomplete healing in his left elbow. LeBron James added another landmark by passing Kareem Abdul-Jabbar for the all-time field goals record, Trae Young made his long-awaited Wizards debut on a minutes limit, and San Antonio's surge stayed central to the league conversation with Victor Wembanyama and the Spurs carrying an elite run. League-wide context also kept shifting through rookie-award movement and trade-deadline evaluations as teams balance immediate impact against longer-term roster bets.
leagueBriefing: The night had a little bit of everything, from playoff-race movement in the West to late-game chaos and milestone history. Minnesota gained ground, San Antonio kept rolling, and Denver held off the Lakers in a game that still became part of LeBron James' legacy resume.

games.headline: Zion and New Orleans' third-quarter burst bury reeling Kings
games.brief: New Orleans weathered an early push, then flipped the game for good with a 15-0 third-quarter run behind Zion Williamson's efficient scoring and all-around floor game. The Pelicans got balanced production and timely shooting to halt a mini-slide, while Sacramento's home misery deepened as its defense again could not string together enough stops.
bullets: Zion Williamson finished with 23 points on 10-for-14 shooting, plus nine rebounds and five assists.
The Pelicans broke it open with a 15-0 run in the third quarter after leading 67-61 at halftime.
Saddiq Bey scored 20 and Trey Murphy III added 21 for New Orleans' secondary punch.
Precious Achiuwa posted 29 points and 12 rebounds, and Russell Westbrook added 19 points and 10 assists for Sacramento.
The Kings dropped to 14-50 and lost their ninth straight at home.
games.context: New Orleans kept pace in the West while Sacramento's season-long slide at home continued.

highlights.title: PELICANS at KINGS | FULL GAME HIGHLIGHTS | March 5, 2026

games.headline: Magic steal it late as Carter's dunk spoils Flagg's return
games.brief: Dallas looked in control in the final minute, but Orlando closed with one final surge and stole the game on Wendell Carter Jr.'s go-ahead dunk with 1.4 seconds left. Cooper Flagg's return gave the Mavericks a spark, yet the Magic's composure in the final possessions and Jalen Suggs' late creation turned a likely Dallas win into a painful one-point collapse.
bullets: Wendell Carter Jr. delivered the winner on a dunk with 1.4 seconds remaining.
Orlando scored the final five points, including Jalen Suggs' clutch 3-pointer with 32.2 seconds left.
Cooper Flagg scored 18 points in his first game back after missing eight with a sprained left foot.
Klay Thompson scored 24 off the bench and hit seven 3-pointers for Dallas.
The Mavericks have now lost 15 of their last 17 games.
games.context: Orlando added a tight late-game win, while Dallas' prolonged skid continued despite Flagg's return.

highlights.title: MAVERICKS at MAGIC | FULL GAME HIGHLIGHTS | March 5, 2026

games.headline: Ace Bailey erupts for 32 as Jazz spoil Trae Young's Washington debut
games.brief: Ace Bailey owned the first half and never let Washington fully recover, dropping a career-high 32 with seven made threes as Utah snapped its own skid. Trae Young's long-awaited Wizards debut delivered flashes, but on a minutes restriction he could not sustain a full comeback, and the Jazz controlled the game's rhythm most of the night.
bullets: Ace Bailey scored a career-high 32 points, with 21 coming in the first half.
Bailey made seven 3-pointers and topped his previous career high of 25.
Trae Young finished with 12 points and six assists in 19 minutes in his Wizards debut.
Juju Reese posted 18 points and 20 rebounds, Washington's top rookie rebounding game this season.
Utah and Washington both entered on seven-game losing streaks, and only Utah snapped theirs.
games.context: Utah halted a long losing run, while Washington's return to health did not stop its slide.

highlights.title: JAZZ at WIZARDS | FULL GAME HIGHLIGHTS | March 5, 2026

games.headline: Heat's second-half surge sends Nets to a 10th straight loss
games.brief: Brooklyn briefly grabbed momentum in the third, but Miami's depth and shot-making quickly overwhelmed it after halftime. Tyler Herro and Bam Adebayo led a balanced attack, the Heat bench flipped the energy, and the Nets' defense unraveled again in another loss that extended one of the league's longest active skids.
bullets: Tyler Herro scored 25 and Bam Adebayo added 21 for Miami.
Miami won bench scoring 54-34 and shot 53% from the field.
Pelle Larsson and Kel'el Ware each scored 16, with Ware adding 11 rebounds and seven blocks.
Michael Porter Jr. led Brooklyn with 27 points.
The Nets lost their 10th straight, their longest skid since an 11-game slide in 2021-22.
games.context: Miami stayed hot in the East while Brooklyn's spiral reached double digits.

highlights.title: NETS at HEAT | FULL GAME HIGHLIGHTS | March 5, 2026

games.headline: Podziemski and Melton grind out overtime win for short-handed Warriors
games.brief: Golden State found just enough late-game execution to survive in overtime, leaning on Brandon Podziemski's timely shot-making and De'Anthony Melton's finishing touch in the final seconds. In a game that swung wildly in the final minute of regulation and overtime, the Warriors closed better, while Houston's late opportunities came up a possession short.
bullets: Brandon Podziemski scored 26 and De'Anthony Melton added 23 in the overtime win.
Melton's tip-in with 5.3 seconds left in OT gave Golden State the cushion it needed.
Stephen Curry missed his 12th straight game with a knee injury.
Reed Sheppard led Houston with 30 points, including six 3-pointers.
The game needed overtime after a chaotic finish that included an overturned foul call in the final seconds of regulation.
games.context: The result nudged Golden State forward in a crowded West race and cost Houston ground in the top-tier seeding battle.

highlights.title: WARRIORS at ROCKETS | FULL GAME HIGHLIGHTS | March 5, 2026

games.headline: Edwards powers Wolves to fifth straight as Minnesota grabs West positioning
games.brief: Minnesota took control with a third-quarter run and never gave Toronto a full path back, with Anthony Edwards setting the tone on both energy and efficiency. The Timberwolves' balanced frontcourt support and clean shot profile carried them to a fifth straight win, and the timing mattered as they gained ground in the Western pecking order.
bullets: Anthony Edwards scored 22 on 8-of-12 shooting and made five 3-pointers.
Minnesota used an 11-0 third-quarter run to build an 86-70 lead.
Rudy Gobert added 18 points and 12 rebounds, while Julius Randle scored 17.
RJ Barrett led Toronto with 25 and Immanuel Quickley had 18.
With Houston losing, Minnesota moved one game ahead of the Rockets for third in the West.
games.context: Minnesota's fifth straight win strengthened its hold on a top-three Western Conference spot.

highlights.title: RAPTORS at TIMBERWOLVES | FULL GAME HIGHLIGHTS | March 5, 2026

games.headline: Wembanyama dominates again as Spurs complete season sweep of Pistons
games.brief: San Antonio controlled this matchup from the opening minutes and leaned on another superstar line from Victor Wembanyama to finish the job. The Spurs never trailed, shot it well early, and kept their surge alive against one of the East's best teams, while Detroit could not recover after falling behind quickly and losing Ausar Thompson to injury.
bullets: Victor Wembanyama posted 38 points, 16 rebounds and five blocks.
The Spurs never trailed and opened the game with a 10-2 burst.
De'Aaron Fox added 29 points and Stephon Castle had 11 points with 12 assists.
San Antonio has won 13 of its last 14 games and swept the season series with Detroit.
Detroit lost Ausar Thompson early to a sprained right ankle.
games.context: San Antonio reinforced its status as a top West contender with another convincing result.

highlights.title: PISTONS at SPURS | FULL GAME HIGHLIGHTS | March 5, 2026

games.headline: Bulls survive late Suns push behind Sexton's 30-point night
games.brief: Chicago looked in command, then had to sweat out a frantic Phoenix comeback in the final minute before escaping with a road win. Collin Sexton carried the scoring load, the Bulls withstood Devin Booker's late shot-making, and Phoenix's final chances to steal it fell short in a game the Suns had seemed poised to flip.
bullets: Collin Sexton scored 30 points to lead Chicago.
Phoenix cut a 12-point deficit to 104-103 on Devin Booker's 3-pointer with 23 seconds left.
Nick Richards hit a late free throw with 4.1 seconds remaining to make it a two-point game.
Booker finished with 27 points in his second game back from a hip injury.
The Bulls won for the second time in three games after an 11-game losing streak.
games.context: Chicago kept a small recovery alive, while Phoenix missed a chance to build on recent momentum in the West.

highlights.title: BULLS at SUNS | FULL GAME HIGHLIGHTS | March 5, 2026

games.headline: LeBron sets another record, but Jokic and Murray carry Denver past Lakers
games.brief: Denver's stars controlled the game's biggest stretches and held off every late Lakers push, but the night still produced a major historical moment when LeBron James became the NBA's all-time field goals leader. Nikola Jokic and Jamal Murray gave the Nuggets enough offense to survive sloppy stretches, and Los Angeles could not quite finish its comeback after James briefly exited with an elbow injury.
bullets: LeBron James passed Kareem Abdul-Jabbar for first in career field goals with his 15,838th make.
Nikola Jokic had 28 points, 12 rebounds and 13 assists for his 23rd triple-double of the season.
Jamal Murray matched Jokic with 28 points, including 20 in the first half.
Luka Doncic scored 27 for the Lakers, who repeatedly cut into Denver's lead but never got in front.
James left briefly after hurting his left elbow, then returned for the closing minutes.
games.context: Denver protected critical ground in a crowded Western playoff race while adding a marquee head-to-head win over Los Angeles.

highlights.title: LAKERS at NUGGETS | FULL GAME HIGHLIGHTS | March 5, 2026
